mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls reduces labor market slack, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.50 percent, the market prices in a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 0.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and increasing nonfarm payrolls signals synchronized economic expansion, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of INDPRO and PAYEMS shows no significant correlation with forward SPX returns (p > 0.10), or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a month-over-month decline in the CPI index signals a disinflationary shock that reduces real interest rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when CPIAUCSL declines during high inflation periods, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness triggers wage inflation pressures that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE drops below 6.0%, or the predictive accuracy of the threshold signal falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in housing starts reflects strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which boosts construction-related corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between HOUST growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observed uptrends.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in real retail sales and a stable federal funds rate indicates robust consumer spending without monetary headwinds, which accelerates equity earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS shows no statistical significance in predicting forward SPX returns, or the combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals pricing power and margin expansion for industrial firms, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive response to INDPRO increases when supply constraints are binding, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
